rulings.Case Title: CAROL SUI MAN LAM et al VS. MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 4, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied, failure to shift burden of proof regarding rescission, and triable issues of fact raised. Memorandum of Fire Insurance is initialed on all pages by both plaintiff and the agent. The term "unrepaired damage" is not explained or defined. Lam described the property as a "fixer upper" which should have prompted further inquiry and investigation. Lam's deposition reflects some lack of knowledge about damage to the property and what should be fixed. (Plaintiffs' UMP #53, 71.) Triable issue of fact regarding the agent's duty to inspect the entire property, (Plaintiffs' UMF 103, Neal deposition) and the agent's checklist (#105, 106). Photos of the property show damage the agent should have reported. (Plaintiffs' UMF 171, Neal deposition.) Triable issue of fact raised whether defendant's investigation was proper and led to a "genuine dispute." =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL SUI MAN LAM et al VS. MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 4, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT AMERICAN SECURITY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied, failure to shift burden of proof. For reasons stated in ruling on Defendant Mid-Century's motion for summary judgment, triable issues of fact raised whether Mid-Century's rescission valid. ASIC does not explain why the issue of whether Mid-Centrury improperly rescinded its policy is relevant to plaintiff's breach of contract claim. Even if Mid-Century improperly rescinded, ASIC has not shown that this would have affect Wells Fargo's actions and requirements with respect to retaining ASIC's insurance for plaintiff's property. The undisputed facts do not show that ASIC's policy was cancelled or otherwise removed based on Mid-Century's purportedly improper rescission. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LINDSEY A HOLMES VS. U.S. BANK NATIONAL DEFAULT SERVICE CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Pltfs' First Amended Complaint, Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 4, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT U.S. BANK, N.A., FKA FIRSTAR BANK, N.A Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. Motion to strike without leave to amend granted as to pragraphs 36, 37, 38, and 39. Statutes referenced not operative until after foreclosure proceedings were concluded. Motion to strike denied as to paragraph 50. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LINDSEY A HOLMES VS. U.S. BANK NATIONAL DEFAULT SERVICE CORP. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 4, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT U.S. BANK, N.A., FKA FIRSTAR BANK, N.A.DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to first cause of action for wrongful foreclosure. No foreclosure occurred and cause of action for attempted foreclosure not recognized in California. No reasonable likelihood defect can be curred by amendment. Demurrer overruled on remaining causes of action. (Court further questions whether date reference in paragraph 54 is a typographical error and should be 2009?). =(302/CWW)
